report_name: Centers Laboratory Data Breach Affects 540,000, Exposing Health Information

threat_actors.name: WorldLeaks
threat_actors.description: A cybercrime extortion group that emerged in 2025, responsible for the data breach at Centers Laboratory. The group operates a dark web leak site where it posts stolen data, and claimed responsibility for stealing 720 GB of data in this incident.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft and extortion targeting the healthcare sector for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The WorldLeaks group accessed and exfiltrated 720 GB of data, including PII and PHI, from Centers Laboratory's internal file servers or databases.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors exfiltrated approximately 720 GB of data, comprising 1.6 million files, from the victim's network over a five-day period.

identities.name: Centers Laboratory
identities.description: A New Jersey-based healthcare testing and diagnostics provider (also known as Centers Lab NJ LLC) that suffered a data breach affecting 542,377 individuals, with PII and PHI being exfiltrated by the WorldLeaks group.

infrastructure.name: WorldLeaks Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the WorldLeaks group to publish information about their victims and the data they have stolen, as seen in the case of Centers Laboratory in October 2025.

indicators.name: Suspicious Large Archive File Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Large Archive File Creation
id: 2b8c1f9a-3d7e-4b0f-9c6a-5d8e4f1a2b3c
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of archive files (zip, rar, 7z), which attackers often use to stage large amounts of data for exfiltration. This rule is behavioral and may require tuning to filter out legitimate administrative activity.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/centers-laboratory-data-breach-affects-540000-individuals/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.zip'
      - '.rar'
      - '.7z'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of large archive files (zip, rar, 7z), which attackers often use to stage data for exfiltration, as suggested in the hunting hints.

indicators.name: Execution of Common Data Exfiltration Tools
indicators.pattern:
title: Execution of Common Data Exfiltration Tools
id: 8e4f1a2b-3c5d-4e6f-7a8b-9c0d1e2f3a4b
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate cloud synchronization tools like rclone and megacmd, which are frequently abused by threat actors for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/centers-laboratory-data-breach-affects-540000-individuals/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megacmd.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of legitimate cloud synchronization tools like rclone and megacmd, which are frequently abused by threat actors for data exfiltration, as suggested in the hunting hints.

relationships.source_name: Execution of Common Data Exfiltration Tools
